skill.name: create-adaptable-composable
skill.description: Create a library-grade Vue composable that accepts maybe-reactive inputs (MaybeRef / MaybeRefOrGetter) so callers can pass a plain value, ref, or getter. Normalize inputs with toValue()/toRef() inside reactive effects (watch/watchEffect) to keep behavior predictable and reactive. Use this skill when user asks for creating adaptable or reusable composables.
skill.summary: Create Vue composables that accept plain values, refs, and getter inputs.
skill.author: vuejs-ai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks as Ruby or shell execution and prose terms as weak cryptography. Manual review found only Vue and TypeScript documentation in SKILL.md, with no executable scripts, shell commands, network access, filesystem access, secret handling, or prompt-injection text.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Alert: Markdown Backticks
low_findings.description: The external command alerts are false positives. The cited lines contain Markdown inline code, TypeScript code fences, Vue API names, and TypeScript examples, not Ruby execution or shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are Markdown documentation and TypeScript examples. No shell interpreter, Ruby code, command substitution, or executable file is present.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Alert: Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts are false positives. The cited lines are frontmatter and prose about composable design, with no cryptographic algorithm, hashing function, encryption call, or credential workflow.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact cited context contains descriptive text such as the skill description and design steps. No crypto API usage or security-sensitive implementation exists in SKILL.md.

content.value_statement: Vue composables often fail when callers pass a mix of plain values, refs, and getters. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to design reusable composables with predictable Vue reactivity.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use MaybeRef and MaybeRefOrGetter in Vue composables.
Guides normalization of inputs with toValue and toRef.
Shows how to place normalization inside watch or watchEffect flows.
Distinguishes read-only inputs from writable two-way inputs.
Warns against treating callback parameters as getters.
limitations: Requires a Vue 3 or Nuxt 3 project.
Does not generate a full package structure by itself.
Does not validate runtime behavior without project tests.
Does not cover Vue 2 composition API compatibility.

use_cases.title: Build Shared Composables
use_cases.description: Create composables that work with plain values, refs, shallow refs, computed refs, and getters.

use_cases.title: Modernize Nuxt Utilities
use_cases.description: Refactor app utilities so Nuxt pages can pass route data, refs, or derived getters safely.

use_cases.title: Review Reactivity APIs
use_cases.description: Check whether a composable API should accept writable refs, read-only getters, or callback functions.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Composable
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new adaptable composable.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Vue 3 composable for this purpose: [describe purpose]. It should accept plain values, refs, or getters when appropriate.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Existing Inputs
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor this Vue composable to use MaybeRef or MaybeRefOrGetter correctly. Explain each input choice and preserve current behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Design Writable State
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a composable that accepts writable reactive state. Use MaybeRef only where mutation is required and document the returned API.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Library-Grade Reactivity
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review before publishing a shared utility.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this composable for library-grade Vue reactivity. Check input types, toValue or toRef usage, watcher placement, callback handling, and edge cases.

output_examples.input: A request to create a document title composable that accepts a string, ref, or getter.
output_examples.output: A short API design, the recommended input type, and an implementation plan using a watcher that reacts to normalized title changes.

output_examples.input: A composable that mutates a count input but currently accepts computed getters.
output_examples.output: A review explaining that writable state should use MaybeRef, with guidance to avoid read-only computed inputs for mutation.

output_examples.input: A utility that accepts a predicate function and was typed as MaybeRefOrGetter.
output_examples.output: A warning that predicate callbacks can be invoked accidentally, plus a safer parameter design.

best_practices: Choose MaybeRefOrGetter for read-only values that may be derived.
Choose MaybeRef only when the composable must write back to the input.
Normalize reactive inputs inside effects so dependency tracking stays correct.
anti_patterns: Do not use MaybeRefOrGetter for callback, predicate, or comparator parameters.
Do not unwrap a reactive input once at setup when later changes must be tracked.
Do not mutate a value that may be a read-only computed ref or getter.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create Vue composables that accept plain values, refs, and getters with predictable reactivity.

faq.question: When should I use MaybeRefOrGetter?
faq.answer: Use it for read-only inputs where callers may pass a plain value, ref, computed ref, or getter.

faq.question: When should I use MaybeRef?
faq.answer: Use it when the composable needs writable input, such as a ref that the composable may update.

faq.question: Does this skill support Nuxt?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance applies to Nuxt 3 projects because they use Vue 3 reactivity.

faq.question: Does this skill run code or install dependencies?
faq.answer: No. It provides implementation guidance and examples for Vue composable design.

faq.question: Can it review an existing composable?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide a review of input types, normalization, watcher usage, and callback handling.
